subject: Human Resources
title: The Remote Work Revolution
description: One-minute skill: The Remote Work Revolution.

hook.text: In February 2020, 6% of Americans worked remotely. By April, it was 35%. A decade of change happened in six weeks.

buildup.text: Remote work existed before COVID—but the pandemic proved it could scale to entire economies almost overnight.

discovery.text: Stanford research found remote workers were 13% more productive—fewer distractions, no commute, and longer focused work time.

twist.text: But isolation, blurred boundaries, and career invisibility emerged—remote work solved some problems while creating new ones.

climax.text: Remote work isn't a temporary experiment anymore—it's a permanent feature of labor market that HR must design for intentionally.

punchline.text: The office didn't die. But it stopped being mandatory.

quiz.question: What did Stanford research find about remote worker productivity?
